member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Codebase.TryLoadAssemblyReferenceFromAssemblyDirectory(System.Reflection.AssemblyName,System.Reflection.Assembly,System.Reflection.Assembly@)
member.summary: This is used to load an assembly reference (dependency) that is located in the same folder as the parent assembly but not loaded by the Editor's AppDomain. The out param dependency can be null if this fails.
member.remarks: This is primarily used for Android builds, see Bug UVSB_2594 and UUM-98843. Ex: Unity.Android.Gradle.dll references NiceIO. NiceIO is not a "scripting" assembly that is explicitly loaded by Unity and is instead loaded dynamically at runtime when some code is jitted that requires the assembly to be loaded. This means the assembly is not in the search path, but is in the same directory as Unity.Android.Gradle.dll. Trying to call Assembly.Load("NiceIO") would fail, but this method would succeed.

member.returns: The new Texture2D instance.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.ReorderableList.IReorderableListDropTarget
member.summary: Can be implemented along with when drop insertion or ordering is desired.

remarks.para: This type of "drop" functionality can occur when the "drag" phase of the drag and drop operation was initiated elsewhere. For example, a custom could insert entirely new items by dragging and dropping from the Unity "Project" window.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ReorderableList.IReorderableListDropTarget.CanDropInsert(System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether an item is being dragged and that it can be inserted or moved by dropping somewhere into the reorderable list control.

remarks.para: This method is always called whilst drawing an editor GUI.

member.returns: A value of true if item can be dropped; otherwise false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ReorderableList.IReorderableListDropTarget.ProcessDropInsertion(System.Int32)
member.summary: Processes the current drop insertion operation when returns a value of true to process, accept or cancel.

remarks.para: This method is always called whilst drawing an editor GUI.
This method is only called when returns a value of true.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ReorderableList.IReorderableListAdaptor.CanDrag(System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether an item can be reordered by dragging mouse.

remarks.para: This should be a light-weight method since it will be used to determine whether grab handle should be included for each item in a reorderable list.
Please note that returning a value of false does not prevent movement on list item since other draggable items can be moved around it.

member.returns: A value of true if item can be dragged; otherwise false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ReorderableList.IReorderableListAdaptor.CanRemove(System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether an item can be removed from list.

remarks.para: This should be a light-weight method since it will be used to determine whether remove button should be included for each item in list.
This is redundant when is specified.

member.returns: A value of true if item can be removed; otherwise false.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection
member.summary: Represents an open connection to a SQLite database.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.#ctor(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Constructs a new SQLiteConnection and opens a SQLite database specified by databasePath.
member.param: Specifies the path to the database file.

Specifies whether to store DateTime properties as ticks (true) or strings (false). You absolutely do want to store them as Ticks in all new projects. The default of false is only here for backwards compatibility. There is a *significant* speed advantage, with no down sides, when setting storeDateTimeAsTicks = true.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.#ctor(System.String,Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteOpenFlags,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Constructs a new SQLiteConnection and opens a SQLite database specified by databasePath.
member.param: Specifies the path to the database file.

Specifies whether to store DateTime properties as ticks (true) or strings (false). You absolutely do want to store them as Ticks in all new projects. The default of false is only here for backwards compatibility. There is a *significant* speed advantage, with no down sides, when setting storeDateTimeAsTicks = true.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.CreateTable``1(Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.CreateFlags)
member.summary: Executes a "create table if not exists" on the database. It also creates any specified indexes on the columns of the table. It uses a schema automatically generated from the specified type. You can later access this schema by calling GetMapping.
member.returns: The number of entries added to the database schema.

member.param: Type to reflect to a database table.
Optional flags allowing implicit PK and indexes based on naming conventions.
member.returns: The number of entries added to the database schema.

member.summary: Creates a new SQLiteCommand. Can be overridden to provide a sub-class.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.CreateCommand(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a new SQLiteCommand given the command text with arguments. Place a '?' in the command text for each of the arguments.
member.param: Arguments to substitute for the occurences of '?' in the command text.
member.returns: A

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.Execute(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a SQLiteCommand given the command text (SQL) with arguments. Place a '?' in the command text for each of the arguments and then executes that command. Use this method instead of Query when you don't expect rows back. Such cases include INSERTs, UPDATEs, and DELETEs. You can set the Trace or TimeExecution properties of the connection to profile execution.
member.param: Arguments to substitute for the occurences of '?' in the query.
member.returns: The number of rows modified in the database as a result of this execution.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.Query``1(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a SQLiteCommand given the command text (SQL) with arguments. Place a '?' in the command text for each of the arguments and then executes that command. It returns each row of the result using the mapping automatically generated for the given type.
member.param: Arguments to substitute for the occurences of '?' in the query.
member.returns: An enumerable with one result for each row returned by the query.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.DeferredQuery``1(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a SQLiteCommand given the command text (SQL) with arguments. Place a '?' in the command text for each of the arguments and then executes that command. It returns each row of the result using the mapping automatically generated for the given type.
member.param: Arguments to substitute for the occurences of '?' in the query.
member.returns: An enumerable with one result for each row returned by the query. The enumerator will call sqlite3_step on each call to MoveNext, so the database connection must remain open for the lifetime of the enumerator.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.Query(Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.TableMapping,System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a SQLiteCommand given the command text (SQL) with arguments. Place a '?' in the command text for each of the arguments and then executes that command. It returns each row of the result using the specified mapping. This function is only used by libraries in order to query the database via introspection. It is normally not used.
member.param: A to use to convert the resulting rows into objects.
Arguments to substitute for the occurences of '?' in the query.
member.returns: An enumerable with one result for each row returned by the query.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.DeferredQuery(Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.TableMapping,System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a SQLiteCommand given the command text (SQL) with arguments. Place a '?' in the command text for each of the arguments and then executes that command. It returns each row of the result using the specified mapping. This function is only used by libraries in order to query the database via introspection. It is normally not used.
member.param: A to use to convert the resulting rows into objects.
Arguments to substitute for the occurences of '?' in the query.
member.returns: An enumerable with one result for each row returned by the query. The enumerator will call sqlite3_step on each call to MoveNext, so the database connection must remain open for the lifetime of the enumerator.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.SaveTransactionPoint
member.summary:
Creates a savepoint in the database at the current point in the transaction timeline. Begins a new transaction if one is not in progress.
Call to undo transactions since the returned savepoint. Call to commit transactions after the savepoint returned here. Call to end the transaction, committing all changes.
member.returns: A string naming the savepoint.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Dependencies.Sqlite.SQLiteConnection.Release(System.String)
member.summary:
Releases a savepoint returned from . Releasing a savepoint makes changes since that savepoint permanent if the savepoint began the transaction, or otherwise the changes are permanent pending a call to .
The RELEASE command is like a COMMIT for a SAVEPOINT.
member.param: The name of the savepoint to release. The string should be the result of a call to

member.summary: Executes within a (possibly nested) transaction by wrapping it in a SAVEPOINT. If an exception occurs the whole transaction is rolled back, not just the current savepoint. The exception is rethrown.
member.param: The to perform within a transaction. can contain any number of operations on the connection but should never call or .

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.TypeSerializer.GetFullNameNoNamespace(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Gets the full name of a type without the namespace.
member.remarks:
The full name of a type nested type includes the outer class type name. The type names are normally separated by '+' but Unity serialization uses the '/' character as separator.
This method returns the full type name of a class and switches the type separator to '/' to follow Unity.
member.param: The full type name, including the namespace.
member.returns: Returns a string.
